title: pleopeltis polypodioides subsp. ecklonii
intro: Pleopeltis polypodioides subsp. ecklonii, also known as the Cape cliff fern, is a type of epiphytic fern native to the southern tip of Africa. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its various medicinal properties, including its ability to reduce inflammation, lower blood pressure, and act as an antiseptic. The health benefits of Pleopeltis polypodioides subsp. ecklonii include its potential to aid in the treatment of respiratory issues, wounds, and skin conditions, as well as its use as a natural remedy for fever reduction. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used topically or taken internally to harness its medicinal properties.
